data.description:

Recent Results Set the Stage for Battalion Oil’s Next Chapter

Battalion Oil (BATL) just posted its latest FY 2025 numbers with Q3 revenue of US$43.5 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.91, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of US$183.4 million and a basic EPS loss of US$3.35. Over the past few quarters, total revenue has moved between US$42.8 million and US$49.7 million while quarterly basic EPS has ranged from a loss of US$1.88 to a profit of US$0.34. This gives investors a mixed read on earnings momentum against a fairly steady top line. With a share price around US$9.14 and reported losses still in the picture, attention now shifts to how production costs, realized commodity prices, and margin pressure shape the risk and reward trade off.

With the headline numbers in place, the next step is to set these results against the most common market narratives around Battalion Oil to see which stories hold up and which get tested by the data.

TTM Loss of US$55.2m Keeps Profitability in Focus

  • Over the trailing twelve months to Q3 FY 2025, Battalion reported total revenue of US$183.4 million and a net loss of US$55.2 million, with basic EPS at a loss of US$3.35.
  • What stands out for a bullish narrative built on improving fundamentals is that, even with this multi quarter pattern of losses, historical loss reduction at a 42.8% annualized rate sits alongside Q3 FY 2025 net income of a US$15.0 million loss. This invites investors to weigh:
    • How a quarterly loss of US$15.0 million compares with earlier periods such as the US$30.9 million loss in Q4 FY 2024 when looking at the overall direction of reported profitability.
    • Whether a share price around US$9.14 paired with an unprofitable trailing twelve month record matches the kind of earnings backdrop bullish holders typically prefer.

Production Costs Around US$23 per BOE

  • In Q3 FY 2025, average production cost per barrel of oil equivalent was US$23.03, set against total oil equivalent production of 1.131 million BOE and realized unhedged prices of US$63.98 for oil, US$0.48 for gas, and US$17.83 for NGLs.
  • Critics highlighting a bearish case built around operational pressure can point to this mix of costs and realized prices, since:
    • Average production cost per BOE stayed in a fairly tight band across recent quarters, from US$24.82 in Q1 FY 2025 to US$22.38 in Q2 and US$23.03 in Q3, while realized oil prices moved between US$69.77 and US$62.14 over the same stretch.
    • Realized gas prices fell from US$1.57 in Q1 FY 2025 to US$0.44 in Q2 and US$0.48 in Q3, which can pressure cash generation when layered on top of continued net losses each quarter.

0.9x P/S with High Share Volatility

  • Over the last 12 months, Battalion traded on a P/S of 0.9x, compared with 1.8x for peers and 2.0x for the wider US Oil & Gas industry, while the share price was also described as materially more volatile than the broader US market over the past three months.
  • What is interesting for investors weighing these risk and reward signals is how the relatively low 0.9x P/S sits alongside the unprofitable trailing twelve month profile and pronounced volatility, because:
    • The TTM net loss of US$55.2 million and basic EPS loss of US$3.35 mean valuation on a P/E or earnings based basis is not available from the data, so the lower sales multiple carries extra weight in the conversation.
    • Share price volatility being higher than the US market over the last three months means that any future change in sentiment around the loss trend or revenue base can move the stock price more sharply than a lower volatility name.

Approach 1: NMI Holdings Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model asks a simple question: given what NMI Holdings earns on its equity compared with the return investors require, how much value is created over time for each share?

For NMI Holdings, book value is $33.98 per share and the stable book value estimate is $43.41 per share, based on weighted future book value estimates from 3 analysts. Stable EPS is $5.91 per share, sourced from weighted future return on equity estimates from 4 analysts. Against a cost of equity of $3.23 per share, this implies an excess return of $2.68 per share.

The model uses an average return on equity of 13.62% and projects value creation on top of that equity base, then capitalizes those excess returns to arrive at an intrinsic value. Under this Excess Returns framework, the estimated fair value for NMI Holdings is $109.90 per share.

Compared with a current share price around $37.20, the Excess Returns model suggests the stock is 66.2% undervalued on this methodology.

Approach 2: NMI Holdings Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful yardstick because it compares what you pay per share with the earnings that each share generates. It gives a quick sense of how much investors are willing to pay for current earnings.

In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E ratio, while lower growth expectations or higher risk tend to align with a lower P/E ratio. So what counts as “normal” depends on both what the business might earn in future and how uncertain those earnings appear to be.

NMI Holdings currently trades on a P/E of 7.27x. That sits below the Diversified Financial industry average P/E of 17.91x and below the peer group average of 8.73x. Approach 1: KKR Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a company is expected to generate above the return that equity investors require, then capitalises those excess profits into an intrinsic value per share.

For KKR, the model starts with a Book Value of $31.81 per share and a Stable EPS estimate of $6.70 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 8 analysts. The Average Return on Equity is 11.94%, while the implied Cost of Equity is $5.23 per share. The difference between what KKR is expected to earn and what equity holders require is the Excess Return, calculated here at $1.47 per share.

Those excess profits are then applied to a Stable Book Value of $56.16 per share, sourced from weighted future Book Value estimates from 3 analysts, to arrive at an intrinsic value of about $81.10 per share. Compared with the current share price of about $90.91, the model suggests KKR is around 12.1% overvalued on this basis.

Result: OVERVALUED

Approach 2: KKR Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to think about value because it links what you pay for each share directly to the earnings that support it. In general, higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth and higher risk tend to align with a lower, more cautious “normal” multiple.

KKR currently trades on a P/E of 36.21x. That sits above the Capital Markets industry average P/E of 29.63x and above the peer group average of 28.97x. Simply Wall St’s “Fair Ratio” for KKR is 24.95x, which is its view of what a reasonable P/E could be given factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and key risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it incorporates company specific characteristics rather than assuming all Capital Markets firms should trade on the same multiple. data.title: Qnity NVIDIA Alliance Puts AI Materials And Newark Ramp In Focus
data.description:
• Qnity Electronics (NYSE:Q) has formed an alliance with NVIDIA to accelerate AI focused research and materials development.
  • The company has opened an advanced manufacturing facility in Delaware dedicated to semiconductor materials for next generation AI and high performance computing.

Qnity Electronics, a supplier of semiconductor materials and components, is tying its growth plans more tightly to AI and high performance computing through this NVIDIA alliance. For investors watching the build out of AI infrastructure, materials suppliers like Qnity sit earlier in the value chain than chip designers or cloud platforms. This positioning can shape how demand flows through the sector. The new Delaware facility adds manufacturing capacity aligned with that role in the supply chain.

For readers tracking NYSE:Q, these updates highlight management’s focus on markets linked to AI related semiconductor demand. The long term impact will depend on how quickly customers adopt new materials and how effectively the company ramps the Delaware facility while working with NVIDIA on AI driven research programs.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Qnity Electronics by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Qnity Electronics.

📰 Beyond the headline: 1 risk and 3 things going right for Qnity Electronics that every investor should see.

The NVIDIA alliance pushes Qnity closer to the center of AI focused materials research, while the Newark facility adds the scale needed to supply those materials into real production. By using NVIDIA tools such as Nemotron, CUDA based simulation, and other modeling platforms, Qnity is tying its materials development to the same compute stack that AI leaders rely on. For you as an investor, that link can matter for design wins, qualification cycles, and how closely Qnity’s products line up with what chipmakers actually need for next generation AI and high performance computing nodes.

Advertisement

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Qnity has a high level of debt, so ramping a 385,000 square foot facility and funding AI focused R&D could weigh on financial flexibility if demand does not materialize as expected.
  • ⚠️ Execution risk around advanced-node materials is real, with strong competitors such as Applied Materials, Lam Research, and Entegris already entrenched with leading foundries.
  • 🎁 The company’s P/E ratio of 35.9x is below the semiconductor industry average of 40x, which some investors may see as a relative value if the AI focused growth story plays out.
  • 🎁 Earnings are forecast to grow 14.05% per year and revenue grew by 9.7% over the past year, which supports the view that Qnity is already tapping into demand for AI and high performance computing related materials.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors should watch how quickly the Newark facility reaches stable output for advanced-node CMP pads, and whether those products gain share in AI oriented chip production. Progress updates on joint projects with NVIDIA, especially around maintaining signal integrity at higher speeds and densities, will be important to see if the collaboration is moving from marketing headline to design wins. It is also worth tracking any commentary on capital spending, debt levels, and utilization rates, because these will indicate whether the expanded footprint is being matched by sustainable orders from chip manufacturers and advanced packaging customers.

A Discounted Cash Flow model projects the cash a business is expected to generate in the future and then discounts those amounts back to what they could be worth today. It aims to provide an estimate of what the entire stream of future cash flows might justify as a share price at the present time.

For KLN Logistics Group, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in HK$. The latest trailing twelve month free cash flow is about HK$2,470.7m, with Simply Wall St using analyst estimates where available and then extrapolating further years. For example, free cash flow for 2024 is projected at HK$2,379.0m, and the extended projections run through to 2035 with gradually changing free cash flow levels over that period.

When these projected cash flows are discounted back, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of HK$13.51 per share, compared with the current share price of HK$6.65. This indicates a 50.8% difference relative to the DCF estimate, suggesting the shares are priced below this cash flow based valuation.

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes the cash that a business is expected to generate in the future, then discounts those amounts back to today to estimate what the company might be worth right now.

For Privia Health Group, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $163.4 million. Analysts have provided detailed forecasts out to 2030, including a projected Free Cash Flow of $204 million in 2030, with later years extrapolated by Simply Wall St beyond the analyst window.

Aggregating and discounting these projected cash flows gives an estimated intrinsic value of about $42.82 per share. Compared to a recent share price around $21.03, the DCF output suggests the stock trades at a 50.9% discount to this estimate, which indicates undervaluation on this specific cash flow view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Privia Health Group Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much investors are currently paying for each dollar of earnings, which helps you compare pricing across different stocks quickly.

What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk profile. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually calls for a lower P/E.

Privia Health Group currently trades on a P/E of 113.49x. That is well above the Healthcare industry average P/E of 21.46x and also above the peer group average of 28.58x. To go a step further, Simply Wall St calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 29.44x for Privia Health Group. This Fair Ratio estimates the P/E that might be reasonable given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks, so it can be more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or sector averages.

With the current P/E of 113.49x sitting far above the Fair Ratio of 29.44x, the stock screens as expensive on this earnings based metric.

Result: OVERVALUED
